finding: Pythia-6.9B (naive4 DPPL +22, outlier ratio 4.6x) retains needle retrieval even at 1413 tokens. Needle retrieval failure requires catastrophic quantization damage (DPPL >200, outlier ratio >8x), as seen only in Qwen2-7B. Moderate PPL degradation does not destroy factual recall.
conclusion: Needle-in-Haystack failure is specific to catastrophic quantization (outlier ratio >8x). nsep+pchan4 achieves perfect retrieval on all models. PPL improvement is the broader contribution; needle rescue is the extreme case.
